Financial Brief · 2026

Operating intelligence
for brand & life.

A multi-surface AI platform. 120+ signups and a growing paying base. Exponential trajectory in the last several weeks. Built solo, with profitable margins on every paid seat.

9
New surfaces · 60 days
Section 02 · Traction

Real signal. Not projections.

Every number below is from the live platform. Subscriptions through Stripe, signups through Firestore, surfaces shipped through git history.

Signups · last 12 months
From zero to 120+, accelerating now.

Word of mouth and a single founder posting in public. No paid acquisition, no SEO, no content team. The curve steepens in the last several weeks as the Brilliance brand transition lands and Founder-tier referrals compound. Funding is for compressing this curve, not creating it.

Free → Paid
10%+
2× prosumer benchmarks.

Industry benchmarks for prosumer SaaS sit at 2–5%. Brilliance is converting north of 10% with no paid funnel. Every paying client is here on intent, not on a coupon. The conversion rate is rising as new Brilliance surfaces expand the value of each paying seat.

Retention
100%
Zero churn to date.

Every paying client from the Founder cohort is still active. Trial-cycle data lands once the first cohort completes month 3.

Stripe · live
Customer mix
Diversified by industry.
Service brands Retreat & hospitality Personal-brand consultants

Names withheld pending NDA review. Concentration risk is low: no single client dominates the paying base.

Validation
Google for Startups Cloud Program
Vetted. Credited. Advised.

Accepted into the Cloud Program with $200K of cloud credits and access to technical advisors. Validation that the platform meets a third-party engineering bar.

Recent revenue signal
Exponential, not linear.

The paying base and MRR are both compounding week-over-week, with subscription deposits running well into the multi-thousand-dollar range across recent cycles. We are intentionally not pinning a static MRR number on this page because that number is moving up faster than a static page can hold. The shape that matters is the curve above, and it is steepening. Funding accelerates this trajectory; it does not create it.

Engagement · sessions per paying client per week
Daily-driver behavior, not weekend tinkering.

Founder-cohort users open Brilliance multiple times per day for chat, Studio runs, calendar, and Pulse goals. Median session count and length will be wired in once roweos_session_log is instrumented in the next deploy. Until then, this card stays honest: "in flight, not faked."

Section 03 · The model

Stress-test the path to $1M ARR.

Editable inputs. The cells on the right recompute in real time. Drag the numbers around. See what's defensible. The math is the same SaaS exit-ARR formula every Series-A deck uses, applied honestly.

Formula: Yr-1 ARR = signups × conv% × ARPU × 12. Y/Y: 3× then 2.5× (standard early-stage SaaS, conservative vs. true PLG at 5–7×). Margin: 92%+ per paid seat (BYOK keeps AI cost near zero on the platform).
Scenario Signups · yr 1 Conv % ARPU $/mo Yr-1 ARR Yr-2 ARR Yr-3 ARR
Floor
Status quo · zero marketing
 $24K $72K $180K
Realistic
Modest content + community
 - - -
Target
Brilliance launch traction · 18 mo plan
 - - -
Stretch
Viral product-led · upside case
 - - -

Cost stack

~$6K/yr Vercel + Firebase + AI providers. Brilliance routes paying users to their own keys, so AI cost is near zero on the platform side. True margin per paying seat: 92%+.

ARPU lift

Blended ARPU rises with upgrades from Solo ($9) to Founder ($29.50) to Premium ($79), plus one-time managed-API-key packs. The $50 baseline is conservative against today's actual mix.

What's not in this model

No team costs (model represents revenue, not P&L). Hiring expense lives in the Use of Funds block below. No enterprise tier ($500+/mo, multi-user). Roadmap, not committed.

Section 04 · The asks

$500K. 18 months. People, not infra.

Round structured as a SAFE. Allocation favors a marketing+content hire to convert organic momentum into a repeatable channel, with engineering and infra in support.

$500K
Round size · SAFE
$5M
Post-money cap
18 mo
Runway target
Brand-side growth marketer + content leadFirst hire · M+1. Goal: 120+ → 5,000 signups in 18 months.
36%$180K
Full-time engineer · Sync v5, mobile native, multi-userSecond hire · M+9. Reduces founder-as-only-engineer risk.
18%$90K
Paid acquisition + content productionNo founder-run paid spend. Run by the marketing hire only.
16%$80K
Infrastructure scaling + GfS extended creditsFirestore, Vercel functions, Veo 3.1 video gen, observability.
12%$60K
Legal + accounting + corporate setupDelaware C-corp conversion, contracts, compliance.
10%$50K
Founder salary @ $40K/yrLean by design. Majority of value remains in equity.
8%$40K
Section 05 · Why & who

Why this build. Why now. Why this founder.

Jordan Rowe
Founder · CEO · Builder

Builder of Brilliance, solo, over 18 months. Single-handed end-to-end: product, engineering, design, brand, support. Previously [experience to fill]. Based in Austin, TX. Operates The Rowe Collection (five real brands: Solo Training, Retreats, Reserve, & Co.) which both fund Brilliance and serve as live test environments for every feature.

Why this build

  • The multi-surface system (Tier 1/2/3) is the differentiator. Most AI products are one chat. Brilliance is nine surfaces (Chat, Studio, Folio, Pulse, Rhythm, Library, Bloom, Notebook, Thought Board), each tuned to a specific intellectual posture.
  • Identity is the moat AI can't sell: it's user-supplied, user-owned. Brilliance learns brand voice and personal context, then injects that identity before the model sees the prompt.
  • Sovereign-by-design. Bring-your-own-key economics let Brilliance run profitably from day one. No VC needed to subsidize unit margins.

Why now

  • Consumer AI hit utility threshold in 2025. The pull is real, not speculative.
  • Most "AI workspaces" target enterprise. The prosumer + small-brand operator segment is wide open: 50M+ creators and SMB owners managing multiple brands with no AI-native operating layer.
  • BYOK economics mean Brilliance can run profitably from a single founder's earnings. We are not raising to keep the lights on. We are raising to compress an 18-month curve into a 9-month curve.
  • Privacy backlash is forming. Sovereign AI (user owns their keys, identity, and data) is positioned for the moment when "AI vendors will train on my data" becomes a category-defining fear.

What v33 already shipped

  • 9 new surfaces in 60 days: Concierge Desk, Focus Mode, Time Ribbon, Letter Series, Split-Pane, Studio at Work, Thought Board, Pinboard, Constellation.
  • 255 tests passing, 0 console errors, 35+ deploys in the last overnight session. Velocity that does not slow down with funding.
  • Brilliance brand identity live across product, marketing, email, PDF, and signup flow as of v33.84 (April 2026).
Risks · the honest version

What could go wrong.

A page with only good news reads like a pitch. Here's what we'd push back on if we were on the other side of the table.

Solo-founder concentration

Bus factor is 1. Single point of decision and execution. Real risk for an investor underwriting durability.

Mitigation: The marketing+engineering hire plan is the explicit answer. First hire (M+1) reduces channel risk; second hire (M+9) reduces engineering risk. Co-founder slot deliberately open for the right person.

AI provider lock-in

If OpenAI or Anthropic vertically integrates an "identity layer," Brilliance's wedge narrows.

Mitigation: User-owned keys route across three providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) at the user's discretion. Identity data is portable. The platform stays neutral as model commodity intensifies.

Distribution · iOS / Android

Brilliance is a PWA today, not a native iOS/macOS app. App Store distribution gates a portion of the prosumer market.

Mitigation: v36 plan is a React Native shell. PWA installs already work on iOS Safari with sync intact. Distribution is a 2027 problem, not a 2026 problem.

Pricing power · enterprise gap

$9–$79/mo positions Brilliance as prosumer. Multi-user / org-tier pricing ($500+/mo) is on the roadmap but not built.

Mitigation: The architecture (per-brand profiles, share codes, multi-tenant data shapes) is already in place. Enterprise tier is a packaging+permissions exercise, not a rebuild.

Take a 30-minute meeting.

Live demo. Live numbers. Live model. Q&A. We don't sell the deck. We show the platform and let it speak.
